title: 3 Under-the-Radar Large-Cap Stocks to Buy in August
text: Diving into three somewhat under-the-radar large-cap stocks that all reported upbeat quarterly results already that investors might want to buy as we head into August.

title: Toast Sets New Standard in Restaurant Management Platforms
text: At first glance, it's easy to mistake Toast, Inc. NASDAQ: TOST as a delivery application like Door Dash Inc. NASDAQ: DASH or an Uber Technologies Inc. NASDAQ: UBER Uber Eats service when placing a restaurant online order. However, it's a complete restaurant ecosystem management platform.

title: Reasons to Add Paychex (PAYX) Stock to Your Portfolio Now

title: CGEMY or PAYX: Which Is the Better Value Stock Right Now?
text: Investors looking for stocks in the Outsourcing sector might want to consider either Cap Gemini SA (CGEMY) or Paychex (PAYX). But which of these two stocks offers value investors a better bang for their buck right now?

title: 4 Factor Dividend Growth Portfolio - Cruising Towards A 20% Return
text: The 4-factor dividend growth portfolio is a strategy that leverages the stock selection process of Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF™, with a few minor twists. The portfolio gained 5.00% in June, underperforming the S&P 500 by 1.60%. Year-to-date, the portfolio is up 11.99% and trailing the S&P 500 by 4.90%. Since inception, the portfolio is generating 3.56% of alpha over the S&P 500.

title: Small Business Jobs Index Dips in June, Rate of Wage Growth Continues to Moderate
text: ROCHESTER, N.Y.--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $PAYX #employmenttrends--The rate of small business hiring slowed modestly in June as hourly earnings growth across the U.S. decelerated to 4.02% from 4.24% in May, according to the Paychex | IHS Markit Small Business Employment Watch. The national Small Business Jobs Index — which measures the rate of small business job growth in the U.S. — registered 99.21 in June, virtually unchanged so far in 2023. “Hourly earnings gains slowed to 4.02%, in line with a gradually slowing rate of inf.

title: My Top 10 High Yield Dividend Stocks For July 2023
text: Despite rising interest rates and stubborn inflation, equities have shown resilience in 2023 with the SPDR S&P Trust ETF adding 6.48% in June and up 16.80% year-to-date. My top 10 list of high dividend yield stocks has generated an annualized rate of return of 13.19% since its inception in November 2020. The list from June posted a solid gain of 5.63%, beating VYM but not SPY.
